title: Geolocated: Russian patrol boat damage near Kerch Strait approaches
summary: ntonc geolocated open-source imagery from the night of April 29-30 confirms damage to Russian patrol craft near the approaches to the Kerch Bridge, consistent with Ukrainian Navy claims of strikes against the FSB Sobol and Grachonok-class anti-sabotage boat.
contents:
# Geolocated: Russian patrol boat damage near Kerch Strait approaches

## Geolocation Confirmation
ntonc's open-source intelligence work geolocated imagery posted to Russian-language Telegram channels from the night of April 29-30 to coordinates near 45.35 N, 36.50 E, immediately east of the Kerch Bridge approaches. Visual matching against landmark structures along the Crimean coast and the bridge support pylons supports a high-confidence geolocation.

## Vessel Identification
Visible features in the imagery include hull silhouettes consistent with the Russian FSB Sobol-class patrol boat and the Grachonok-class anti-sabotage boat referenced in Ukrainian Navy claims. ntonc rates equipment identification confidence as HIGH for the Sobol-class and MEDIUM-HIGH for the Grachonok, pending higher-resolution overhead imagery.

## Methodology and Caveats
ntonc cross-referenced four Telegram posts, two milblogger commentaries and a Ukrainian Navy press statement. The thread notes that final battle-damage assessment requires either Ukrainian after-action overhead imagery or Russian Black Sea Fleet acknowledgment. Some impact-pattern interpretation remains pending higher resolution imagery.

## Tags
ntonc, ukraine, russia, kerch-strait, geolocation, fsb, sobol, grachonok, osint
